title: Pick rate-limit strategy for /checkout endpoint
context: We just saw a burst of 5x normal traffic from one IP block. Need a guard before tomorrow's promo. Auth is per-user JWT. Redis already in stack.

options.label: Token bucket per user_id (Redis)
evidence: per-user fairness preferred over per-IP for logged-in users
options.predicted_next_step: add middleware checkout.ts with redis token bucket, default 30 req/min per user, burst 60
options.cost_if_wrong: false-positive blocks of legit power users; reversible by raising limit

options.label: IP-based fixed window in nginx
evidence: but shared NAT users get punished
options.cost_if_wrong: punishes corporate NAT users; reload nginx to revert

options.label: Do nothing, watch dashboards
evidence: rate limit could cause more incidents than abuse
options.predicted_next_step: add alert at 10x normal, manual triage
options.cost_if_wrong: DB outage if abuse continues; pager event
